title: The Velvet Mask
blurb: Infiltrate a crime-lord's masquerade to steal the ledger that would hang him.

masque_landing.scene: Candlelight, string music, and a sea of feathered masks. Tonight crime-lord Dorian Vael throws his autumn masquerade, and somewhere in his private study sits the Ashford Ledger — proof that would hang him, and the reason you talked your way through the gate. A liveried doorman, Crispin, guards the way into the ballroom proper; he is bored, bribable, and waiting to hear tonight's passphrase. A narrow servants' stair drops away unwatched toward the kitchens. (Crispin will wave through anyone who gives the night's word — but he will not, of course, tell you what it is.)

the masked doors into the ballroom.denied: Crispin's gloved hand settles on the door. 'The word, friend, or the stair's that way.' He nods toward the servants' steps — he will not budge without tonight's passphrase.

ballroom.scene: A glittering crush of dancers beneath a hundred candles. Vael's personal bodyguard, a scarred giant of a man named Bront, watches the room from beside the study's gilded doors — not yet hostile, but reading every face that drifts too close. A flute of wine and a careless word could turn him, or a clever one could draw him off his post. On a side table, a guest has abandoned a Vael house-token beside a half-empty glass.

kitchens.scene: Steam, clatter, and the reek of roasting fowl. Two of Vael's roughnecks lounge by the dumbwaiter, half-drunk and arguing over dice, their backs to the larder you slipped in through — they have no idea anyone is here. The dumbwaiter shaft climbs straight up to the study above; a back stair offers the longer, quieter way.

study.scene: A warm, book-lined sanctum that smells of cigar smoke and old money. Dorian Vael himself stands at the hearth, masque pushed up onto his brow, a duelist's rapier already loose in his hand — he heard you coming. The Ashford Ledger sits in plain sight on the desk between you. He is a businessman before he is a killer; there may yet be a price, or a bluff, that ends this without blood.
